name: 1983 Chinese Cuisine

notes: Every frozen row was reconciled against the current restaurant-issued website menu and the restaurant-linked Toast Main Menu, Morning Dim Sum, and Togo All Day surfaces.
Ninety-three frozen rows are current menu items; the remaining row, Noodles & Fried Rice, was a section heading emitted as an item.
The corrected catalog contains 169 deduplicated current rows across real sections, with beverages ordered after food and dessert.
The restaurant does not publish a complete allergen matrix; no allergen absence or cross-contact safety claim is made.
Positive signals are retained only where current restaurant-controlled menu titles explicitly name an allergen-bearing ingredient or species.

Twenty-three frozen rows had incorrect or incomplete signals; unsupported cake-wide wheat/egg assumptions and coconut-as-tree-nut were removed, while explicit fish, mollusk/crustacean, soy, egg, milk, sesame, peanut, and tree-nut terms were restored.
The frozen output assigned every item the generic category chinese and shifted adjacent headings or item names into variantGroup, which made the displayed menu structure incorrect.

The frozen extraction also emitted a section heading as an item, duplicated equivalent products across menu surfaces, omitted current menu rows, and overclassified allergens from broad dish-name heuristics.

The correction parses real menu boundaries, deduplicates current surfaces, keeps the direct Toast page at restaurant_linked_vendor authority, and leaves the text retrieval proxy at third_party authority.

sourceAttempts.outcome: Raw non-browser retrieval returned Cloudflare HTTP 403; the public browser-rendered page remained accessible and was reviewed directly.
notes: A text-render proxy was retained only as a third-party reproducibility aid and was not promoted to restaurant-linked authority.

findings.summary: All 94 frozen rows had a generic category, adjacent headings/items bled into variantGroup, one section heading was shown as an item, and 76 current deduplicated items were absent.
findings.resolution: Rebuilt a 169-row current catalog from both restaurant-controlled menu surfaces, using actual categories and deduplicating equivalent rows.

findings.summary: Twenty-three of 93 real frozen menu rows had incorrect or incomplete allergen signals.
findings.resolution: Removed unsupported name-based assumptions and rebuilt positive signals only from explicit current menu terms; all other allergens remain unavailable.

findings.summary: The direct Toast page is restaurant-linked but raw HTTP capture is Cloudflare-blocked; a third-party text render was needed for reproducible parsing.
findings.resolution: Verified the direct link and rendered page separately, recorded Toast as restaurant_linked_vendor, and retained the proxy strictly as third_party evidence.

repairs.summary: Prepared and targeted-tested a 169-item current snapshot with real categories, deduplicated surfaces, beverages last, and conservative source-backed allergen signals.
verificationCommands: RESTAURANT_REPOSITORY_PATH=tmp/restaurant-verification/1983-repair-input.json node scripts/repair-final-generated-regressions.mjs (targeted copy; verified 169 unique items, nine real category groups, 63 direct signals, 106 unavailable rows, and beverages last)

notes: Current snapshot counts: 225 raw Toast rows plus 95 official-site rows reconciled to 169 current deduplicated items; 63 have an explicit positive allergen signal and 106 remain allergen-data unavailable.
Focused catalog, reconciliation, targeted repair-copy, unique-id, category-order, and source-semantics checks passed.
